behavior_summary: This script implements the interaction and short-speech behavior for a generic 'peasant' NPC. It manages what happens when the player looks at, uses (interacts with), or targets/casts on the NPC, and it ensures interaction state is cleared when the player leaves the NPC's fast-trigger area.
U8P_PESANT1::leaveFastArea is run when the avatar exits the NPC's fast-area trigger and clears several stored interaction references so the NPC stops being considered engaged or targeted.

U8P_PESANT1::use is the player-interact entry: it can temporarily refuse interaction (setting a somebodyTalking flag and playing a short refusal line) or dispatch to one of two small dialogue routines (U8P_PESANT1::func0656 or U8P_PESANT1::func030D) depending on the NPC's map id.

U8P_PESANT1::cast handles being targeted/attacked: it runs a short timed loop while no target is present, and if a target is detected it calls guard-response logic and may shout for help (randomized).
U8P_PESANT1::look returns a short description: 'dead peasant' if dead, otherwise 'peasant'.

U8P_PESANT1::func030D and U8P_PESANT1::func0656 are in-script dialogue flows that reset interaction refs, present two-option menus, U8P_PESANT1::use urandom to choose greeting/branch variants, handle player choices, and restore NPC activity when finished.
U8P_PESANT1::func0B02 is a thin wrapper that delegates to U8P_PESANT1::func0656.
notable_control_flow: U8P_PESANT1::leaveFastArea: deterministic reset sequence — calls FREE::resetRef several times to clear engagement refs then ends.

U8P_PESANT1::use: checks item frame for a specific value (frame 0x14) to refuse conversation (sets somebodyTalking, spawns a timer helper, barks refusal, clears flag). If not refused, picks a dialogue based on Npc::getMap and calls either U8P_PESANT1::func0656 or U8P_PESANT1::func030D.

U8P_PESANT1::func030D/U8P_PESANT1::func0656: both build a menu array, enter a ConversationLoop that Ask + SuspendAssigns the player's choice into a local, then strcmp on that choice to select responses. Both U8P_PESANT1::use urandom to choose greeting/response variants and spawn a process to call Npc::cSetActivity(0x00, this) when the conversation ends.

U8P_PESANT1::cast: when invoked it loops with a small counter (local1) spawning FREE::func1C19 and suspending until either the counter expires or Npc::getTarget(this) indicates a target. If a target is present it calls CALGUARD::guardRespondToCrime and may bark for help unless the NPC is dead.
open_questions: FREE::func1C19 is spawned repeatedly in multiple places (U8P_PESANT1::cast, U8P_PESANT1::use refusal, conversations). It appears to be a short-timer helper process for scheduling/suspend behavior, but its precise effects (timing durations, side-effects) are not visible here.
The resetRef IDs (0x0205, 0x0204, 0x00F0, 0x0200) are used to manage interaction state; the exact meaning of each ID (which reference they represent) is not explicit in the script.

somebodyTalking is written in U8P_PESANT1::use() as a transient busy marker; whether other scripts or NPCs read this flag (to globally suppress conversations) is not shown — does it act as a local region lock or a global conversation mutex?
The U8P_PESANT1::cast(uword) parameter exists but is not observed in the recovered nodes; its intended U8P_PESANT1::use (spell id, source id) is therefore uncertain.

Map id thresholds used in U8P_PESANT1::use() to choose dialogue (examples: 0x27, 0x28, 0x29) are present in the code; the mapping between map ids and social/contextual variants of dialogue is inferred but not exhaustively enumerated.

main_function.name: U8P_PESANT1::leaveFastArea
main_function.purpose: Clear stored interaction/engagement references when the avatar or actor exits the NPC's fast-area trigger so the NPC is no longer targeted or considered engaged.
entry_conditions: Triggered by the engine when the avatar (or relevant actor) leaves the NPC's fast-area region (U8P_PESANT1::leaveFastArea event).
Runs unconditionally on that event and performs multiple FREE::resetRef calls to clear refs.

function_map.name: U8P_PESANT1::leaveFastArea
function_map.role: fast-area exit handler: clear interaction references/cleanup

function_map.name: U8P_PESANT1::look
function_map.role: U8P_PESANT1::look/examine handler: returns short description ('peasant' or 'dead peasant')

function_map.name: U8P_PESANT1::cast
function_map.role: target/U8P_PESANT1::cast handler: timed loop while idle, alert guards and shout for help when attacked/targeted

function_map.name: U8P_PESANT1::use
function_map.role: player 'U8P_PESANT1::use' / interaction entry: refuse if busy or route to dialogue variants

function_map.name: U8P_PESANT1::func030D
function_map.role: dialogue flow A: small greeting/menu (two-option), handles choices and restores activity

function_map.name: U8P_PESANT1::func0656
function_map.role: dialogue flow B: personalized name reply + menu with branching/insult variants, restores activity

internal_calls.purpose: Run the dialogue variant (U8P_PESANT1::func0656) for certain maps or default path after a successful U8P_PESANT1::use interaction
Run the alternate dialogue variant (U8P_PESANT1::func030D) for specific maps when the player interacts
Simple delegation/alias to start the U8P_PESANT1::func0656 dialogue

external_calls.purpose: Clear stored interaction references for this NPC (several IDs), to remove engagement/targeting state
Alert guards / trigger guard response when the NPC reports a hostile target
Spawn a short helper/timer process used to pace repeated behavior or timing between suspends
Inspect the player's held item/frame to decide whether to refuse interaction (special-case frame 0x14)
Clear/update specific interaction refs at the start of conversation to mark engagement cleanly
Schedule restoration of the NPC's idle/activity state after finishing a conversation
Clear/update interaction refs at dialogue start and during the conversation
Obtain the NPC's name to compose a personalized menu option ('Why, I am <name>!')
Restore the NPC's idle/activity state at conversation end
Spawn helper/timer process during conversation for timing/suspend control
Spawn a helper/timer process when refusing to talk (used together with somebodyTalking flag and a suspend)

writes.name: somebodyTalking
writes.likely_effect: Transiently mark that an NPC/region is busy refusing or temporarily engaged so other interactions should be suppressed; set to 1 during a short refusal/timed pause and cleared to 0 afterwards.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: This script implements the interaction and short-speech behavior for a generic 'peasant' NPC. It manages what happens when the player looks at, uses (interacts with), or targets/casts on the NPC, and it ensures interaction state is cleared when the player leaves the NPC's fast-trigger area.
